concerns.text: Citation 'gemini_3_1_pro_2026' (lines 14, 108, 112) and 'openai2026introducing_gpt5_4' (line 14) refer to unreleased/future-dated models (2026). The paper claims these models drive context scaling, but no public technical report or preprint exists for these specific versions to verify the '128K and beyond' claim. Verify if these are placeholders or if the citation keys map to existing 2024/2025 reports (e.g., Gemini 1.5 Pro).

Citation 'gao2025prolong' (lines 105, 133, 156) is used to contrast LLM long-context practices. Verify that this specific 2025 citation exists and supports the claim that LLMs typically mix short data, as the paper argues pure long-data works for LVLMs. If this is a placeholder for a 2024 paper (e.g., Prolong-2024), update the key and year to ensure factual accuracy.

Citation 'zhang2026browsecomp' and 'zhang2026clawbench' (line 12) are dated 2026. Verify these are not placeholders. If these are future works, the claim that they 'enable' current capabilities is factually unsupported. Replace with existing 2024/2025 benchmarks if available, or clarify the citation status.

Claim in Abstract (line 24) states 'improves long-document VQA scores by 7.1%'. Table 1 (line 338) shows an improvement from 50.59 to 57.70, which is a 7.11 absolute point increase. The text says '7.1%' which could be interpreted as a relative percentage increase (which would be ~14%). Ensure the unit 'absolute percentage points' is used to avoid ambiguity, or verify the calculation matches the intended metric.
